text: @Pewdiepower II It's funny, you somehow search my comments and avoid all the arguments I've won xD
author: @Resanctify

text:
@Pewdiepower II I've told you before just because it's an emergency or a catastrophe doesn't mean they can't be abused and used to take over.
No party should be allowed to change your rights based on "oh there is an emergency". The major thing holding those power grabbers back is the U.S. and the U.N.( whose big strength is the U.S.) wagging a no no finger. When China finishes cannabalizing the U.S. buying all the land and companies, all you other countries are sitting ducks. Pray to every imaginary god that Russia takes your country over China.

True Freedom of speech is better than any other countries On and off restricted speech.
The only thing You don't like about True freedom of speech is that people can disagree with you and criticize your ideas/actions.
You sound pretty much like a potential dictator.
author: @Resanctify

text:
@Pewdiepower II  On dictatorships, once any leader has any real control of the local, state or national police, or can control the military, there's nothing you or your family or governers or senators can do if those newly in power decide to disappear their closest political competition, silently.
It's debatable which militaries would stop if ordered to attack their own country, the U.S. military would not, but cops in the u.s. are just people who sought power, they have no allegiance to the people, they can come from foreign families and can choose their own police department because they are not required to like the country or the people, most of them just like power.
In a snap moment you grant one person power, there is no transparency no 24/7 live feed of what they are doing, they will do whatever they want to do, carefully.
As for insults, no country anywhere should criminalize insults, enforcing niceness is not something you want.
People Now hate fake nice people.
As for Discrimination, that only applies to Employment and Services, businesses and employers cannot discriminate based on those things.
There is no restriction on what people can talk about or insult about, southern people aren't put in jail for saying the word or insulting hispanic people and african americans aren't at risk of anything after massively calling all white people scum on twitter.
YouTube keeps taking down posts, this is why censorship of any kind is bad, you can't even discuss people who commit crimes in your neighborhood because someone may get offended.
author: @Resanctify

text:
@UCD7YT-_jwYm8Qz5wlM2MvPA I'm a classic liberal, as in everything liberal but With Freedom of Speech.
When I say dictatorships can happen in a flash, I don't mean the U.S., the U.S. has Real freedom of speech and real power distribution.
The division of power is only an illusion in most countries, whoever controls the military in those countries can wipe out the pretend division.
You cannot remove hate, in fact, not letting people get angry and call you a name for something you did can force them to plan a more elaborate response.
The human race is not evolved, a wife or husband who find their spouse cheating still turn into spiteful petty cavemen physically and legally. If we were evolved there wouldn't be so many people betrayed and then stomped on by "loved ones".
Someone who sees you stealing their favorite open parking space may want to rip your head off.
You cannot get rid of this and criminalizing insults is enforcing niceness.
In your world, what happens when someone gets mad at you and calls you a name, do they get fined? What happens when they don't have enough for the fine or don't want to pay for something you caused? Do they go to jail?
author: @Resanctify

text:
@Pewdiepower II You keep making walls of texts with feelings "you feel..."  "you think that....".
Let's get real clear on facts, it is not illegal or harassment if someone yells a racial slur or calls someone too old or someone tells a woman to go back to the kitchen.
None of these are illegal, if it becomes Ongoing, as in the person keeps yelling like a maniac, the cops might cite them for disturbing the peace, which is small potatoes.
If someone targets someone continually, some sensitive person can try to sue for harassment, but those ususally get thrown out if it was just at a public space one time.
Let's get that completely clear before I start reading your walls of text full of opinions.
author: @Resanctify
